NOTICE OF ASSIGNMENT OF JUDGMENT BACK TO THE ORIGINAL JUDGMENT CREDITORS: ELMO DE NEVI AND VIRGINIA DE NEVI (TRANSACTION ID # 54998720) FILED BY ASSIGNEE RANDOLPH BUSH DBA APPLIED JUDICIAL RESEARCH AS TO DEFENDANT CHAN, BILLY KIM KUN CHAN, ALICE SUK NGAN

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS BILLY CHAN AND ALICE CHAN'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND REINSTATE FILINGS IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW THAT THE JUDGMENT IS VOID, I.E. COURT LACKED POWER TO HEAR OR DETERMINE THE CASE. PROTECTIONS OF SURETY LAWS ARE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE, WHICH HAVE NOT BEEN PLEAD BY THE MOVING PARTY. ADDITIONALLY, MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT ASSIGNOR OF THE LEASE IN A SURETY WHERE IT CONTRADICTS EXPRESS TERMS OF THE ASSIGNMENT AND THERE IS NO KNOWLEDGE OF ANY SURETY AGREEMENT BY THE LANDLORD. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BILLY CHAN AND ALICE CHAN'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND REINSTATE FILINGS IS CONTINUED TO APRIL 3, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN MARCH 6, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BILLY CHAN AND ALICE CHAN'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AND REINSTATE FILINGS. NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 501, THE REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING COURT, TO BE HEARD ON APRIL 4, 2014 AT 9:30 AM. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

PROCEEDINGS NOT REPORTED. (514)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Continued To Jul-07-15 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 514

Defendants Billy Chan And Alice Chan'S Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Reinstate Filings
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Emmanuel Enyinwa, Esq., Attorney at Law, (415-956-6100), for Billy Kim Chan Kun, Defendant and moving party.
Gina E. Sharron, Esq. of Rentschler/Tursi LLP, (650-524-1980), for Elmo De Nevi, Plaintiff and opposing party.

Argued by both parties. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendants Billy Chan and Alice Chan's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment And Reinstate Filings is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the judgment is void, i.e. court lacked power to hear or determine the case. Protections of surety laws are affirmative defense, which have not been plead by the moving party. Additionally, moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that assignor of the lease in a surety where it contradicts express terms of the assignment and there is no knowledge of any surety agreement by the landlord. Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)

MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Court Trial/Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Judge: Donald J. Sullivan, Clerk: S. Ravinantapricha, CSR: Sonia Wong #11288.
Ben Sange, Esq. for plaintiffs Elmo De Nevi and Virginia De Nevi.
Plaintiff's Witness Virginia De Nevi was sworn and direct examined.
Default(s) of Defendants Billy Kim Kun Chan and Alice Suk Ngan Chan has been duly entered.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendants as follows:

Damages/Principal - $25,127.49
Prejudgment Interest - None requested.
Attorney's Fees - $7,911.80
Costs - $664.00, for a total judgment in the amount of $33,703.29

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.
